tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58355,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Update error handling docs for eager validation and structured error fields","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58341) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58341 introduced two major behavioral changes to the Responses server: 1. **Eager pre-handler validation**: `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are now validated against the provider *before* `CreateAsync` is invoked. Invalid references now return HTTP 404/400 immediately instead of being silently ignored. The resolved history items are cached so handlers that later call `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse the prefetched result. 2. **Structured error field propagation**: `ResourceNotFoundException` gained `Code` and `Param` properties (mirroring `BadRequestException`). `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts all four fields (`message`, `code`, `param`, `type`) from Foundry error JSON and propagates them through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404 upstream error codes are normalized to HTTP 500 (never proxied). ## Gaps Found - **README.md — Troubleshooting section** does not mention that invalid `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` values are rejected with 400/404 before the handler runs (users expect this to be silent or handler-handled) - **README.md — Troubleshooting section** does not mention that non-400/404 errors from upstream Foundry storage are normalized to HTTP 500 - **docs/handler-implementation-guide.md — Handler Exceptions table** still shows `ResourceNotFoundException` → `error.code: null`, but it now carries `Code` and `Param` from the exception (matching `BadRequestException` behavior) - **docs/handler-implementation-guide.md — Validation Pipeline section** does not document the new pre-handler validation step for `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit Locate the **Troubleshooting** section (~line 183) and extend the common-errors list to include the new pre-handler validation errors and the 500-normalization behavior: ```markdown ## Troubleshooting ### Common errors - **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as `model` (when provided) are valid and that `input` items are well-formed. - **400 Bad Request** (`conversation.id`): The `conversation.id` field referenced a conversation that the provider rejected as invalid. This validation runs before your handler is invoked. - **404 Not Found** (`previous_response_id`): The `previous_response_id` field referenced a response that does not exist or has expired. This validation runs before your handler is invoked; the handler is never called when the reference is invalid. - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. - **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response was not created with `background=true`, or it has already reached a terminal state. - **404 Not Found** (cancel): The response was created with `store=false`, or a non-background response is still in-flight and not findable. - **500 Internal Server Error**: An unexpected error occurred server-side. Errors from the upstream Foundry storage layer that are neither 400 nor 404 are always returned as HTTP 500 — the original upstream status code is never proxied to clients. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit **Change 1 — Handler Exceptions table**: Update the `ResourceNotFoundException` row to reflect that `Code` and `Param` are now propagated (lines around 1134): Replace the existing table: ```markdown | Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.message` | |---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|----------------| | `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from exception (e.g., `"invalid_value"`) | from exception | | `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | `null` | from exception | | `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception | | Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `"server_error"` | `"An internal error occurred."` | | `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | ``` With: ```markdown | Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.param` | `error.message` | |---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|----------------| | `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from `Code` (e.g., `"invalid_value"`) | from `ParamName` | from exception | | `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | from `Code` | from `Param` | from exception | | `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception | from exception | | Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `"server_error"` | `null` | `"An internal error occurred."` | | `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | ``` **Change 2 — Validation Pipeline section**: Extend the section (~line ~1172) to document the new pre-handler validation: After the existing paragraph that begins _"Bad client input returns HTTP 400 before your handler runs"_, add: ```markdown **Pre-handler reference validation**: In addition to payload schema validation, the library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the storage provider *before* `CreateAsync` is called: - An unrecognised `previous_response_id` causes the library to return HTTP 404 immediately; your handler is never invoked. - An invalid `conversation.id` causes the library to return HTTP 400 immediately; your handler is never invoked. When the references are valid the resolved history items are cached, so `GetHistoryAsync()` inside your handler reuses the prefetched result without issuing a second storage call. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are needed for this change — the additions are prose and a table update. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Troubleshooting** section content needs updating. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README diffs from snippet injection 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24652253628/agentic_workflow) · ● 695.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58355","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T06:47:10Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:47:10Z"}

{"number":58355,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Update error handling docs for eager validation and structured error fields","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58341) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58341 introduced two major behavioral changes to the Responses server: 1. **Eager pre-handler validation**: `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` are now validated against the provider *before* `CreateAsync` is invoked. Invalid references now return HTTP 404/400 immediately instead of being silently ignored. The resolved history items are cached so handlers that later call `GetHistoryAsync()` reuse the prefetched result. 2. **Structured error field propagation**: `ResourceNotFoundException` gained `Code` and `Param` properties (mirroring `BadRequestException`). `StorageErrorMapper` now extracts all four fields (`message`, `code`, `param`, `type`) from Foundry error JSON and propagates them through the exception hierarchy. Non-400/404 upstream error codes are normalized to HTTP 500 (never proxied). ## Gaps Found - **README.md — Troubleshooting section** does not mention that invalid `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` values are rejected with 400/404 before the handler runs (users expect this to be silent or handler-handled) - **README.md — Troubleshooting section** does not mention that non-400/404 errors from upstream Foundry storage are normalized to HTTP 500 - **docs/handler-implementation-guide.md — Handler Exceptions table** still shows `ResourceNotFoundException` → `error.code: null`, but it now carries `Code` and `Param` from the exception (matching `BadRequestException` behavior) - **docs/handler-implementation-guide.md — Validation Pipeline section** does not document the new pre-handler validation step for `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — Edit Locate the **Troubleshooting** section (~line 183) and extend the common-errors list to include the new pre-handler validation errors and the 500-normalization behavior: ```markdown ## Troubleshooting ### Common errors - **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as `model` (when provided) are valid and that `input` items are well-formed. - **400 Bad Request** (`conversation.id`): The `conversation.id` field referenced a conversation that the provider rejected as invalid. This validation runs before your handler is invoked. - **404 Not Found** (`previous_response_id`): The `previous_response_id` field referenced a response that does not exist or has expired. This validation runs before your handler is invoked; the handler is never called when the reference is invalid. - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist or has expired past the configured TTL. - **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response was not created with `background=true`, or it has already reached a terminal state. - **404 Not Found** (cancel): The response was created with `store=false`, or a non-background response is still in-flight and not findable. - **500 Internal Server Error**: An unexpected error occurred server-side. Errors from the upstream Foundry storage layer that are neither 400 nor 404 are always returned as HTTP 500 — the original upstream status code is never proxied to clients. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` — Edit **Change 1 — Handler Exceptions table**: Update the `ResourceNotFoundException` row to reflect that `Code` and `Param` are now propagated (lines around 1134): Replace the existing table: ```markdown | Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.message` | |---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|----------------| | `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from exception (e.g., `"invalid_value"`) | from exception | | `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | `null` | from exception | | `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception | | Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `"server_error"` | `"An internal error occurred."` | | `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | ``` With: ```markdown | Exception Type | HTTP Status | Response Status | `error.code` | `error.param` | `error.message` | |---------------|-------------|-----------------|-------------|--------------|----------------| | `BadRequestException` | 400 | `failed` | from `Code` (e.g., `"invalid_value"`) | from `ParamName` | from exception | | `ResourceNotFoundException` | 404 | `failed` | from `Code` | from `Param` | from exception | | `ResponsesApiException` | from exception | `failed` | from exception | from exception | from exception | | Any other exception | 500 | `failed` | `"server_error"` | `null` | `"An internal error occurred."` | | `OperationCanceledException` | *(special)* | `cancelled` | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | *(see [Cancellation](#cancellation))* | ``` **Change 2 — Validation Pipeline section**: Extend the section (~line ~1172) to document the new pre-handler validation: After the existing paragraph that begins _"Bad client input returns HTTP 400 before your handler runs"_, add: ```markdown **Pre-handler reference validation**: In addition to payload schema validation, the library validates `previous_response_id` and `conversation.id` against the storage provider *before* `CreateAsync` is called: - An unrecognised `previous_response_id` causes the library to return HTTP 404 immediately; your handler is never invoked. - An invalid `conversation.id` causes the library to return HTTP 400 immediately; your handler is never invoked. When the references are valid the resolved history items are cached, so `GetHistoryAsync()` inside your handler reuses the prefetched result without issuing a second storage call. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are needed for this change — the additions are prose and a table update. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections (Getting started, Key concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing). Only the **Troubleshooting** section content needs updating. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm no README diffs from snippet injection 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24652253628/agentic_workflow) · ● 695.7K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58355","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T06:47:10Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:47:10Z"}

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4280857957,"number":58268,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"[AgentServer] Docs: Document beta.2 features in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses README","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` **Triggered by:** PR #58252 and release commit cc01a6c by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58252 added the following significant features to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`, all released in 1.0.0-beta.2: - **Chat isolation key enforcement**: new `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header that, when supplied on `CreateResponse`, must be repeated on all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls. Mismatches silently return 404 for security isolation. - **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: a new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). - **Metadata validation constraints**: metadata maps are now validated for ≤ 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 chars, value length ≤ 512 chars. - **Malformed response ID validation**: both `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are validated for format; malformed values return 400. - **Error response shape corrections**: cancel-after-delete now returns 404 (was 400); `invalid_request_error` type is now used consistently. ## Gaps Found - The **Key concepts** section of the README has no mention of `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`. Users who need multi-tenant isolation have no guidance on how to use it or what the header enforcement contract is. - The **Troubleshooting / Logging** section says only "The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source." It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or how to enable logging of outbound Foundry storage calls for diagnostics. - The **Troubleshooting / Common errors** section incorrectly says cancel returns `400 Bad Request` for terminal-state responses, and doesn't mention that cancel-after-delete returns **404**. It also doesn't mention the new **400** for malformed response IDs. - No documentation exists for the metadata constraints (16-key, 64/512-char limits) that now produce validation errors.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit** #### 1a. Add a "Chat isolation" subsection under Key concepts After the existing `### Thread safety` subsection, add: ```markdown ### Chat isolation The `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enables multi-tenant isolation. When a response is created with this header, all subsequent operations for that response — GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems — must supply the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns `404 Not Found` (indistinguishable from a non-existent response) so that cross-tenant enumeration is not possible. When no isolation key is set at creation time, no enforcement is applied to subsequent calls. ``` #### 1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Common errors section Replace the existing bullet list with: ```markdown ### Common errors - **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as `model` (when provided) are valid, that `input` items are well-formed, that `metadata` contains ≤ 16 keys with key length ≤ 64 characters and value length ≤ 512 characters, and that any supplied `previous_response_id` matches the expected ID format. - **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The `response_id` path parameter or the `previous_response_id` request body field does not match the expected format (prefix `caresp_` followed by 50 characters). - **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). The response body contains an `invalid_request_error` error type with the message `"Cannot cancel a response in terminal state."`. - **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL, was created with `store=false`, or was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` that does not match the key provided in this request. - **404 Not Found** (cancel or delete): Returned when the response does not exist *or* has already been deleted — cancel-after-delete returns 404 per spec. ``` #### 1c. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section Replace the existing paragraph under `### Logging` with: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. #### Foundry storage call logging When the Responses library is backed by Azure AI Foundry storage (`FoundryStorageProvider`), `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound HTTP call at `Information` level on success and `Warning`/`Error` level on failures. Each log entry includes: - HTTP method and URI - HTTP status code and latency - Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id` (outbound), `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, and `apim-request-id` (inbound from Foundry) To enable these logs, configure the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging `Information` level for `Azure.AI.AgentServer` in your `appsettings.json`: ```json { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer": "Information" } } } ``` ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new snippet test files are required for these changes — the documentation additions are explanatory prose, not code samples. However, if a sample demonstrating chat isolation is desired, add a new test file: **Path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample5_ChatIsolation.cs` Wrap the illustrative code in `#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion` and place the corresponding snippet placeholder in the README's new "Chat isolation" subsection. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` already contains all required sections. The changes above update existing sections and add one new subsection; no sections need to be created from scratch. Current structure (all present): 1. ✅ Getting started — Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server 2. ✅ Key concepts — needs "Chat isolation" subsection added 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting — needs Logging and Common errors updated 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — only needed if new snippet regions were added 4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > Docs: Document InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware and new Responses logging features","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (primary); also `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` **Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/` **Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58274) by `@RaviPidaparthi` ## What Changed PR #58274 added the following features to the AgentServer packages: 1. **`InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`** (Core, `beta.22`) — a new auto-registered ASP.NET Core middleware that logs every inbound HTTP request with method, path, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`, OpenTelemetry trace ID). Successful requests log at `Information`; 4xx/5xx at `Warning`. Query strings are deliberately excluded to avoid logging sensitive data. 2. **Deterministic `agent_session_id` derivation** (Responses, `beta.2`) — SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"` truncated to 63 lowercase hex chars; falls back to random hex when no conversational context is available. 3. **Handler-level structured logging** (Responses, `beta.2`) — `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints (POST, GET, Cancel, Delete, InputItems) with response ID, conversation ID, and store flag. ## Gaps Found - The **Core README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`) makes no mention of `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware`. It only says traces come from the Responses/Invocations activity sources, which is now incomplete and misleading — users may not realize all HTTP requests are automatically logged. - The **Core README Key concepts section** does not list `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` or describe the built-in HTTP-level logging as a default server behavior. - The **Responses README Logging section** (`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md`) does not mention handler-level structured logs or `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, leaving users unable to discover what log categories/levels to configure. - The **Responses README Key concepts section** does not document `agent_session_id` derivation — developers building conversation-aware handlers need to understand that session IDs are deterministically derived from agent name, version, and partition hint, and what the fallback behavior is.
📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files #### File 1: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` — edit In the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Health endpoint`: ```markdown ### Request logging `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` is registered automatically and logs every inbound HTTP request to the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` logger category: | Event | Log level | Fields | |-------|-----------|--------| | Request started | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` | | Request completed (2xx/3xx) | `Information` | `Method`, `Path`, `StatusCode`, `DurationMs`, `XRequestId`, `ClientRequestId`, `TraceId` | | Request failed (4xx/5xx) | `Warning` | Same as above | Query strings are intentionally excluded from all log messages to avoid logging sensitive data. To adjust the minimum log level, configure the category in your `appsettings.json`: ```json { "Logging": { "LogLevel": { "Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal.InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware": "Warning" } } } ``` ``` In the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text: ```markdown ### Logging The library registers `InboundRequestLoggingMiddleware` automatically, which emits structured `Information`-level log messages for every inbound HTTP request and `Warning`-level messages for 4xx/5xx responses. Correlation headers (`x-request-id`, `x-ms-client-request-id`) and the OpenTelemetry trace ID are included in every log entry for cross-service request tracing. The library also emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` and `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Invocations` activity sources. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose startup issues. ``` #### File 2: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — edit In the **Key concepts** section, add a new subsection after `### Thread safety`: ```markdown ### Session ID derivation Each response is assigned a deterministic `agent_session_id` derived from a SHA-256 hash of `"{agent_name}:{agent_version}:{partition_hint}"`, truncated to 63 lowercase hex characters. The partition hint is extracted from the `conversation_id` or `previous_response_id` in the request. When no conversational context is available, a random hex value is used instead. The derivation priority is: 1. `agent_session_id` provided explicitly in the request payload 2. `AGENT_SESSION_ID` environment variable 3. Deterministic SHA-256 hash (when conversation context is available) 4. Random 63-char hex (fallback) ``` In the **Troubleshooting → Logging** section, replace the existing text: ```markdown ### Logging The library emits structured `Information`-level logs on all Responses API endpoints. Each log entry includes the `ResponseId` and, where applicable, `ConversationId`, `PreviousResponseId`, `Model`, `Streaming`, `Background`, `Store`, `Status`, and `OutputCount` fields: | Endpoint | Log details | |----------|-------------| | POST `/responses` | response ID, model, streaming, background, store, conversation ID, previous response ID | | POST `/responses` (completed, non-streaming) | final status and output count | | GET `/responses/{id}` | retrieved status and output count | | POST `/responses/{id}/cancel` | result status | | DELETE `/responses/{id}` | success flag | | GET `/responses/{id}/input_items` | response ID | The `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound Foundry storage API call at `Information` level with HTTP method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers. OpenTelemetry traces are emitted via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source. Enable ASP.NET Core logging in your application configuration to diagnose storage and handler issues. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new code snippets are required for these documentation-only additions. All content is conceptual description and configuration examples that do not need snippet extraction. ### Step 3: Verify README structure Both READMEs already contain all required sections in the correct order: 1. ✅ Getting started (Install the package, Prerequisites, Configure the server) 2. ✅ Key concepts — **needs new subsections added** as described in Step 1 3. ✅ Examples 4. ✅ Troubleshooting — **Logging subsections need updating** as described in Step 1 5. ✅ Next steps 6. ✅ Contributing ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 2. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` 3. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 4. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/` 6. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/` No snippet update or API export is needed since these are documentation-only changes.
## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > arguments.message: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

arguments.body:
## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** A bot-generated documentation gap issue for `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` requesting updates to README troubleshooting content and the handler-implementation-guide to reflect new eager pre-handler validation behavior and structured error field propagation introduced in PR #58341.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`
**Affected API:** README.md (Troubleshooting section), `docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` (Handler Exceptions table + Validation Pipeline section)
**Scenarios:**
- Users expect `previous_response_id` / `conversation.id` validation to be silent or handler-handled, but it now returns HTTP 404/400 before the handler is invoked
- `ResourceNotFoundException` now carries `Code` and `Param` properties (matching `BadRequestException`), but docs still show `error.code: null`
- Non-400/404 upstream Foundry storage errors are normalized to HTTP 500, but this is not documented

**Root ask:** Update README troubleshooting section and handler-implementation-guide to document the new pre-handler validation and structured error fields.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

This is a documentation-only gap — no code behavior changes are needed. The implementation guide in the issue body provides precise file locations and diff content for both changes.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Review PR #58341 (commit [REDACTED]) to confirm the behavioral changes described
2. Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` Troubleshooting section with pre-handler validation errors and 500-normalization note
3. Update `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/docs/handler-implementation-guide.md` Handler Exceptions table and Validation Pipeline section to reflect `Code`/`Param` on `ResourceNotFoundException`
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — The issue is about SDK documentation and behavior, not REST API or service-side issues; not a management or provisioning package
- **Service:** `AI Agents` — Package is `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` in `sdk/agentserver/`; confirmed by prior issues #58268 and #58293 using the same label pair
- **Confidence:** High — Package name and service directory unambiguously map to a single service label; category is clearly "Client"
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %AI Agents` (line 85) — matched because predicted service label is "AI Agents"
- **AzureSdkOwners:** none listed
- **ServiceOwners:** @ankitbko @RaviPidaparthi
- **Routing action:** Added "Service Attention" label; issue is bot-filed (not customer-reported) so "needs-team-attention" was not added; @mentioned ServiceOwners via routing comment
- **Scan notes:** Scanned bottom-to-top from end of CODEOWNERS; no multi-label entries involving "AI Agents" were found; matched at line 85 on first relevant `%AI Agents` entry
</details>
